user_claim: Customer: Morning. I parked near office and later noticed something off in the front. | Agent: Is this about one part or multiple parts? | Customer: Two things, I think. The front bumper looks damaged and the left headlight also looks affected. | Agent: Should we review both as part of this claim? | Customer: Yes, front bumper and left headlight together.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The bumper and headlight are each shown in close-up clear enough to judge the claimed conditions.
claim_status_justification: img_3 shows the left headlight intact, illuminated, and undamaged, contradicting the headlight damage claim; img_2 shows only minor surface scuff/scratches on a bumper, and the three images depict differently styled white vehicles (img_1 is a driving Maruti SUV) suggesting non-original/stock content inconsistent with a single damaged car.

user_claim: Customer: Need to file a car damage claim. | Agent: What part of the car? | Customer: Door. | Agent: Scratch, dent, or paint issue? | Customer: A deep dent on the door panel. It was not there before.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The car door panel is clearly visible at an angle that allows the dent to be assessed.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a visible dent/deformation on the door panel near the body seam, with a finger pointing to it, consistent with the claimed dent on the door.

user_claim: Customer: A stone hit the front glass while driving. | Support: Are you reporting the windshield? | Customer: Yes. It looks shattered from my side. | Support: Any other part involved? | Customer: No, only the windshield shatter claim.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the windshield from inside the car with visible impact damage, allowing the shatter claim to be assessed.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows multiple stone-impact star fractures and radiating cracks across the windshield consistent with the stone-strike shatter claim. img_2 only shows an interior driving view and is not needed.
